title: Used to / Be used to / Get used to

items.question: I ___ play the piano when I was a child.
explanations: Correct! A past habit takes 'used to + base verb': 'used to play'.
'Am used to' means accustomed, not a past habit.
'Get used to' means becoming accustomed.
'Used to playing' is wrong for a past habit.

items.question: She works nights, so she ___ sleeping during the day.
explanations: Correct! Accustomed = 'be used to + -ing': 'is used to sleeping'.
'Used to sleeping' isn't the past-habit structure.
'Use to' is not correct here.
'Used sleeping' is not correct.

items.question: Don't worry — you'll ___ the new software soon.
explanations: Correct! Becoming accustomed = 'get used to + noun': 'get used to the software'.
'Used to' is a past habit.
'Be use to' is not correct.
'Get used' is missing 'to'.

items.question: There ___ a cinema here, but it closed years ago.
explanations: Correct! A past state takes 'used to + base verb': 'used to be'.
'Is used to be' is not correct.
'Gets used to be' is not correct.
'Used to being' isn't the past-state structure.

items.question: I'm not ___ this cold weather yet.
explanations: Correct! 'Be used to + noun': 'I'm not used to this cold weather'.
'Use to' is not correct here.
'Used' alone is missing 'to'.
'Using to' is not correct.

items.question: It took a while, but he ___ living alone.
explanations: Correct! Became accustomed = 'got used to + -ing': 'got used to living'.
'Used to living' isn't correct for this meaning.
'Was use to' is not correct.
'Get used' is missing 'to' and the wrong tense.

items.question: We ___ go camping every summer.
explanations: Correct! A repeated past habit: 'used to go camping'.
'Are used to' means accustomed.
'Get used to' means becoming accustomed.
'Used to going' is wrong for a past habit.

items.question: After a month, I ___ the long commute.
explanations: Correct! Became accustomed: 'got used to the long commute'.
'Used to' would mean a finished past habit.
'Am use to' is not correct.
'Used to commuting' isn't the right structure here.

items.question: Correct this: 'I'm used to work at night.'
options: I'm used to working at night.
I used to working at night.
I'm use to work at night.
explanations: Correct! After 'be used to', use -ing: 'used to working'.
'I used to working' mixes two structures.
'I'm use to work' is not correct.
'Used to work' after 'be' is wrong (that's a past habit).

items.question: Which structure describes a FINISHED past habit?
explanations: Correct! 'Used to + base verb' = a finished past habit.
'Be used to + -ing' means accustomed.
'Get used to + -ing' means becoming accustomed.
'Be used to + noun' means accustomed to something.
